content.full_text:
.
[SEAL: CITY OF GLENDORA]                                          FPPC
                                                    Oct 10 9 27 AM '91
                    CITY OF GLENDORA    CITY HALL              (818) 914-8201
                    116 EAST FOOTHILL BLVD., GLENDORA, CALIFORNIA 91740

ARTHUR E. COOK
CITY MANAGER

October 7, 1991


Bianca Breeze, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Re:    Request for Advice of the Commission under
       Section 83114 of the Government Code

Dear Ms. Breeze:

This letter is written in my capacity as City Manager for the City of Glendora and is
intended to serve as a request for advice pursuant to the provisions of Section 83114
of the Government Code. The request for advice is made on behalf of, and with the
authorization of, Bob Kuhn, a member of the Glendora City Council, and Robert C.
Odell, Dale Colby and Sue Bauer, members of the Glendora Planning Commission.

This request for advice is made relative to the following facts:

The City of Glendora is undertaking a comprehensive review of its entire General Plan.
Public hearings by the Planning Commission and then by the City Council are
scheduled to begin within the next month or so. Based on the proposed
recommendations of a citizen advisory committee, it appears likely that the present
land use designations of up to 100 parcels may be changed.

One of the parcels under consideration for a change of land use designation lies
within a redevelopment project area and is situated immediately adjacent to the
Glendora Country Club, which is a non-profit corporation that owns and operates a
private golf course and clubhouse facility. The approximately 488 members own an
equity interest in the Club, the present value of which is approximately $35,000 per
membership. Members may sell their memberships as long as the prospective buyers
are approved by the Club. Councilmember Kuhn and Commissioners Odell, Colby
and Bauer are members of the Club.

In light of scheduling of the upcoming public hearings on the proposed revised
General Plan, your advice relative to potential conflicts of interest is requested as
follows:

1.    Does an equity membership in the Glendora Country Club constitute an
      interest in real property which presents a potential conflict of interest for
      the Councilmember and the Commissioners?


      P R I D E    O F    T H E    F O O T H I L L S

2.

In the event it is determined that the Councilmember and the
Commissioners have a conflict of interest due to their Club membership,
do the definitions of "public generally" and "significant segment of the
public" as contained in Section 87102.6 apply?

3.

In the event it is determined that Section 87102.6 is not applicable, does
the prohibition against participation extend only to the above-described
parcel or to all 100 parcels?

4.

If it is subsequently determined that a land use designation change to
one or more of the other 100 parcels may have a material financial effect
on other property interests that may be owned by the Councilmember or
one of the Commissioners, does that alter your advice to that individual
pursuant to items 2 and 3 above?

Thank you for your consideration of these issues. If you require additional information
in order to prepare a response, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely,

Arthur E. Cook
City Manager

jj

sections.question:
1. Does an equity membership in the Glendora Country Club constitute an interest in real property which presents a potential conflict of interest for the Councilmember and the Commissioners?

2. In the event it is determined that the Councilmember and the Commissioners have a conflict of interest due to their Club membership, do the definitions of 'public generally' and 'significant segment of the public' as contained in Section 87102.6 apply?

3. In the event it is determined that Section 87102.6 is not applicable, does the prohibition against participation extend only to the above-described parcel or to all 100 parcels?

4. If it is subsequently determined that a land use designation change to one or more of the other 100 parcels may have a material financial effect on other property interests that may be owned by the Councilmember or one of the Commissioners, does that alter your advice to that individual pursuant to items 2 and 3 above?
sections.question_synthetic: The City of Glendora requests FPPC advice regarding whether equity memberships in the Glendora Country Club constitute a conflict of interest for city officials voting on General Plan revisions that may affect an adjacent parcel, and whether conflict-of-interest prohibitions extend to all 100 parcels under review or only the adjacent parcel.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or advice section present. All four legal questions are clearly enumerated in the document. Document is well-structured with clear facts and numbered questions.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request from the City of Glendora's City Manager to the FPPC requesting advice on conflict of interest issues for city council and planning commission members who are members of the Glendora Country Club and will be voting on a comprehensive General Plan review that may affect a parcel adjacent to the Club.
